facade_colour: warm red brick with buff/cream brick voussoirs and string course accents

hcd_data.architectural_style: House form, Detached, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The property retains most of its original building features including its cross-gabled roofline, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, first storey bay window, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched opening. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. The property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red brick with buff/cream brick voussoirs and string course accents; prominent front-facing gable
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Brick in good condition; polychromatic brickwork well preserved; cross-gable form intact
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor: 2 windows in projecting bay with segmental arches. Second floor: 2 windows with buff brick voussoirs. Gable peak has small attic window.
photo_observations.door_details: Entrance beside bay window with small stoop
photo_observations.cornice_details: none visible; bargeboard in gable area
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Three-sided projecting bay spanning ground and first floors; classic Bay-and-Gable
photo_observations.porch_details: Low concrete stoop with steps, wooden porch structure visible.
photo_observations.chimney_details: Single brick chimney visible at roof ridge.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Polychromatic brickwork with buff voussoirs and string courses; decorative bargeboard in gable
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Minimal alterations; retains original Bay-and-Gable form; some replacement windows likely
photo_observations.notes: Classic Bay-and-Gable Victorian. Polychromatic brickwork well preserved.

notes: Classic Bay-and-Gable Victorian. Polychromatic brickwork well preserved.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, detached, bay-and-gable typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window, string courses, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: The property retains most of its original building features including its cross-gabled roofline, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, first storey bay window, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched opening. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Row two-storey bay-and-gable in warm red brick with buff/cream brick voussoirs and string course accents under a cross-gable roof. Features include a double-height three-sided projecting bay, decorative bargeboards, string courses and polychromatic brickwork. Minimal alterations; retains original Bay-and-Gable form; some replacement windows likely. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: The property retains most of its original building features including its cross-gabled roofline, brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, first storey bay window, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched opening. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
